steps.title: Quick question first…
steps.question: Electric current is the flow of…
options: Charge (electrons move from negative to positive)
steps.misconceptionFeedback: ⚠️ Common misconception! Current is the rate of flow of charge. Electrons (which carry the charge) actually move from negative to positive — the opposite of conventional current direction. Current is NOT the flow of energy or voltage.
steps.correctFeedback: ✓ Correct! Current is the rate of flow of charge. Electrons move from negative to positive (the opposite of conventional current direction shown on circuit diagrams).
steps.bestSource: BEST resource: AQA Physics specification 4.2.1 — Electric charge and current

steps.title: Charge and Current
steps.bodyHTML: Electric current is the rate of flow of electrical charge. In metals, current is carried by electrons. Electrons flow from negative to positive, but conventional current is drawn from positive to negative. The amount of charge that flows depends on the current and the time.

relationships: Bigger I → more charge flows each second
Longer t → more total charge flows

commonErrors: Confusing electron flow direction with conventional current direction
Forgetting to convert minutes to seconds before using Q = It
Mixing up charge (Q, coulombs) with current (I, amps)

steps.title: See it in action
steps.instruction: Drag the sliders to change current and time. Watch how charge builds up.

aqa.questionText: A current of 0.5 A flows through a lamp for 2 minutes. Calculate the charge that flows through the lamp.

steps.label: Identify the knowns
steps.content:
current I = 0.5 A
time t = 2 minutes = 120 s

steps.label: Apply Q = It
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — correct substitution; 1 mark — correct answer with unit

edexcel.questionText: A battery delivers a current of 3.0 A for 5.0 minutes. Calculate the total charge that flows.

steps.label: Identify the knowns
steps.content:
current I = 3.0 A
time t = 5.0 minutes = 300 s

steps.label: Calculate charge
steps.markSchemeNote: 1 mark — substitution; 1 mark — correct answer + unit

aqa.questionText: A current of 2.5 A flows through a resistor for 3 minutes. What is the total charge that flows?
aqa.markSchemeAward: 1 mark conversion · 1 mark substitution · 1 mark correct answer + unit
aqa.commonMistake: Option A is a trap — using 3 minutes instead of 180 seconds gives 7.5.

equation.examTip: Always convert time to seconds before substituting. 1 minute = 60 s.

boardNotes.aqa: On AQA formula sheet. Must be able to rearrange for I or t.
boardNotes.edexcel: On Edexcel equation sheet. Remember to convert minutes to seconds.
boardNotes.ocr-gateway: On OCR Gateway formula sheet. Check unit conversions carefully.
boardNotes.wjec: Provided in WJEC exam. Know all three rearrangements.
